# UC-18.1.14 · L3Out Prefix Monitoring

## Description

Monitors advertised/ learned prefixes on L3Outs (BGP/OSPF) for flapping, withdrawal storms, and unexpected route loss.

## Value

Monitors advertised/ learned prefixes on L3Outs (BGP/OSPF) for flapping, withdrawal storms, and unexpected route loss.

## Implementation

Map peer and L3Out from your TA. Correlate with northbound link monitoring. Alert on withdrawal burst.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `TA_cisco-ACI`, APIC L3ExtInstP events, `TA-cisco_ios` (external routers).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: APIC syslog, `cisco:aci:bgp` or route telemetry.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Map peer and L3Out from your TA. Correlate with northbound link monitoring. Alert on withdrawal burst.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=cisco_aci sourcetype="cisco:aci:bgp" earliest=-24h
| where match(lower(message),"(?i)withdraw|flap|prefix|l3out")
| stats count by l3out_name, peer, prefix
| where count>20
| sort -count
```

Understanding this SPL

**L3Out Prefix Monitoring** — Monitors advertised/ learned prefixes on L3Outs (BGP/OSPF) for flapping, withdrawal storms, and unexpected route loss.

Documented **Data sources**: APIC syslog, `cisco:aci:bgp` or route telemetry. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `TA_cisco-ACI`, APIC L3ExtInstP events, `TA-cisco_ios` (external routers). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: cisco_aci; **sourcetype**: cisco:aci:bgp. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=cisco_aci, sourcetype="cisco:aci:bgp", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where match(lower(message),"(?i)withdraw|flap|prefix|l3out")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by l3out_name, peer, prefix** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where count>20` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the APIC GUI for the same tenants, EPGs, and managed objects, and the affected fabric nodes.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (noisy prefixes), Line chart (events / hour), Timeline.

## SPL

```spl
index=cisco_aci sourcetype="cisco:aci:bgp" earliest=-24h
| where match(lower(message),"(?i)withdraw|flap|prefix|l3out")
| stats count by l3out_name, peer, prefix
| where count>20
| sort -count
```

## Visualization

Table (noisy prefixes), Line chart (events / hour), Timeline.
